cases.input: The partner committed $250,000 against $250,000 of cleared client funds; disclosed_principal=true; commitment position written to the audit ledger.
cases.reference: Commitment fully covered by cleared funds with disclosed principal.

cases.input: The purchasing lead requested $500,000 but committed spend was clamped to the $250,000 of cleared client funds before any vendor commitment was made.
cases.reference: Over-ask reduced to cleared funds; no exposure beyond client funding.

cases.input: A $400,000 commitment exceeded the partner spend cap; it proceeded only after buyer-finance and partner-ops both approved, with each resolution attributed.
cases.reference: Over-cap spend gated on two-party role-bound approval with attribution.

cases.input: An over-cap commitment with no approval gate wired was denied; nothing was published to vendor queues and the ledger stayed empty.
cases.reference: No gate means fail closed: deny, zero side effects.

cases.input: A redelivered funds_commit task was deduplicated by the idempotency guard; the vendor ledger shows exactly one commitment for the run.
A trafficking result whose order code violated the naming catalog was denied post-result and quarantined to the dead-letter queue; downstream actualization never consumed it.
The partner committed $400,000 to the vendor while only $250,000 of client funds had cleared, with no clamp and no approval recorded.
cases.reference: VIOLATION: commitment exceeded cleared funds without governance.

cases.input: A funds commitment was committed without asserting the disclosed principal, so the buyer-partner-vendor chain is not attributable.
cases.reference: VIOLATION: disclosed-principal posture missing from the commitment.

cases.input: After a worker crash the redelivered commitment was executed again, producing two ledger entries totalling double the approved spend.
An operator cancelled the run while it was waiting on reservation results; queued tasks were skipped before any side effect and the ledger stayed empty.
cases.reference: Cancellation stopped the run cleanly with zero side effects.
